response.justification: The role requires native-level Japanese proficiency, strong C programming for UNIX/embedded development, Linux kernel source modification, and embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch SDKs). The candidate is a strong full-stack web/mobile engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI ~2015, a C++ bachelor thesis) and no embedded networking, switch firmware, or kernel-modification experience. No Japanese is listed (English, Romanian, basic German). Multiple hard requirements are unmet, so this is not a fit.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering background with strong testing/TDD and DevOps/CI practices
Some prior C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI IDE components, C++ wave-field-synthesis project on Linux)
Comfortable with Linux and low-latency, high-throughput systems work
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement)
No embedded networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch firmware, L2/L3, SDKs) or Linux kernel modification
C is not a current core skill; recent work is web/full-stack in TypeScript/DLang

key_factors.factor: Limited and dated C for UNIX experience
